blocks.speaker: Ms. Barrett
blocks.content: We already have one wide receiver. One thing, please.

blocks.speaker: Ms. Barrett
blocks.content: That's okay. Take your time. We'll circle back.
She kept moving. The class moved with her. I sat in what I'd just said — or what I hadn't said.

blocks.speaker: Maya
blocks.content: You gave us less than everyone else.
She'd turned around just enough. Not dramatically — like she'd done the math and decided the angle was worth it.

blocks.speaker: Larry
blocks.content: Does Ms. Barrett know you just turned around?

blocks.speaker: Maya
blocks.content: I don't know you well enough to say that yet.
She faced forward. Ms. Barrett kept going. And I thought: that was a real conversation. In the middle of class introductions with twenty-two people in the room. We just had a real conversation.
Jonathan caught my eye from across the row. Raised an eyebrow. Looked at Maya. Looked back at me. Nodded one slow time like he was confirming something.

blocks.prompt: After class. Maya is packing up slowly. She's not rushing.

options.label: Wait for Jonathan. Let him make the introduction.
Head out first. See if she follows.
